response:
Short version — by following the Service Bus operational logs back to the client and then matching that to app configuration.

Steps & evidence:
- The Service Bus operational logs showed repeated Failed Retrieve Subscription operations with ErrorCode Unauthorized (SubCode=40100). The raw event we used had TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 and EventTime 2026-04-15 16:48:51Z.
- Operational logging was only forwarded to blob storage (ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70), not Log Analytics, so we searched the raw blob logs (insights-logs-operationallogs) for that hour and found the exact payload: EventName "Retrieve Subscription", Status "Failed", Caller "ServiceBus Client", Via "sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/pim-dev/Subscriptions/check-reader?...".
- The same hour showed hundreds of repeated failures (~442–450 attempts each) against five pim-dev subscription management endpoints (check-reader, check-contributor, get-eligibility, request-reader, request-contributor).
- We inspected namespace/topic auth rules: only RootManageSharedAccessKey existed at namespace level; the pim-dev topic had a topic-scoped SAS rule named "pim" with rights Listen and Send but NOT Manage — management ops like GetSubscription require Manage, explaining the 40100.
- We checked Azure app inventory and app settings. pim-backend-dev had a SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION_STRING in its settings that pointed to sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/ with SharedAccessKeyName=pim and EntityPath=pim-dev, which exactly matched the failing entity and SAS key in the logs. pim-api did not match (it was configured for pim-prod).
- Conclusion: the unauthorized 40100 GetSubscription calls were coming from a Service Bus client (very likely pim-backend-dev) using a topic-scoped "pim" SAS (Listen/Send only) while making management calls that require Manage rights.

Recommended immediate fixes (brief):
- Stop using the message-bus SAS for admin/management calls; use namespace-level Manage credentials or AAD/RBAC, or remove the GetSubscription calls if unnecessary.
- Move connection strings out of app settings into Key Vault.
context_injected:
--- Episode 1 [codex 2026-04-15] (similarity: 0.707) ---
On 2026-04-15, traced Service Bus 40100 unauthorized GetSubscription errors in namespace prod-azure-v3 to a specific client pattern and likely source app. Service Bus operational logs were only configured to Datadog forwarding storage, not Log Analytics, so the raw blob logs in storage account ddlogstorage3a0c161f2e70 had to be searched. The exact payload with TrackingId 7b845798-c613-49fa-9cc3-c49ca97cce31_G17 was found in insights-logs-operationallogs for 2026-04-15 hour 16. Matching record: EventTimeString 4/15/2026 4:48:51 PM +00:00, EventName 'Retrieve Subscription', Status 'Failed', Caller 'ServiceBus Client', Via 'sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/pim-dev/Subscriptions/check-reader?api-version=2021-05&enrich=True', ErrorCode Unauthorized, ErrorMessage SubCode=40100 Unauthorized access for GetSubscription. Same hour showed repeated failures from ServiceBus Client against five pim-dev subscription management endpoints: check-reader, check-contributor, get-eligibility, request-reader, request-contributor, with roughly 442-450 attempts each in that hour. Service Bus namespace auth rules showed only RootManageSharedAccessKey at namespace level. Topic pim-dev had topic-scoped SAS rule 'pim' with rights Listen and Send only, no Manage. This exactly explains the 40100 on GetSubscription, which is a management operation. Azure app inventory showed web apps pim-api (prod) and pim-backend-dev (dev). pim-api was configured for SERVICE_BUS_TOPIC_NAME=pim-prod and did not cleanly match the failures. pim-backend-dev app settings directly contained SERVICE_BUS_CONNECTION_STRING pointing to Endpoint=sb://prod-azure-v3.servicebus.windows.net/ with SharedAccessKeyName=pim and EntityPath=pim-dev, making it the probable source of the repeated unauthorized calls. Conclusion: the 401s are coming from a Service Bus client, very likely pim-backend-dev, using the topic-scoped 'pim' SAS for pim-dev on the prod-azure-v3 namespace, and attempting admin GetSubscription calls that require Manage rights. Recommended fixes: stop using the message-bus SAS for admin subscription lookups; either use a separate admin client with namespace-level Manage credentials or AAD/RBAC Data Owner, or remove the GetSubscription checks if not needed. Also move the connection string to Key Vault if it remains in app settings.

--- Episode 2 [codex 2026-03-20] (similarity: 0.5743) ---
On 2026-03-20, live Azure investigation of Log Analytics workspace DefaultWorkspace-5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661-SCUS in subscription 5ad08c08-96bb-45c0-a567-8bcc86020661 (Azure Engineering - Production Subscription) found AppExceptions billed usage of 1552.998 GB over 90 days. 95.5% of this was from App Insights component prod-azure-audit-engine / Function App prod-azure-audit-engine in resource group ussc-prod-audit-engine-rg001. Dominant signature: Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus.ServiceBusException at AmqpConnectionScope.CreateReceivingLinkAsync with 101,940,682 rows and 1483.83 GB from 2026-01-28 onward at ~25-34 GB/day. Sample messages showed MessagingEntityNotFound 404s against missing Service Bus entities in namespace fazure-janus-sb-primary, especially partner-center-agreements/Subscriptions/pc-agreements-sub (~646.37 GB), arc-lh-deployment/Subscriptions/arclhdeploymentsub (~645.78 GB), decommissioning-events/Subscriptions/decom-process (~95.02 GB), and decommissioning-events/Subscriptions/fetch-ri-instances (~94.95 GB). Verified topics partner-center-agreements and arc-lh-deployment do not exist. Verified function app settings still reference these names: agreementDateSBTopicName=partner-center-agreements, agreementDateSBSubscriptionName=pc-agreements-sub, LHServiceBusTopicName=arc-lh-deployment, LHSubscriptionName=arclhdeploymentsub, DecomProcessServiceBusTopicName=decommissioning-events, DecomProcessSubscriptionName=decom-process, FetchRISubscriptionName=fetch-ri-instances. Activity log shows restarts/host sync by vikesh.agarwal@rackspace.com on 2026-01-29, aligning with onset window.

--- Episode 3 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.5454) ---
On 2026-03-19, read-only inspection of the prod Service Bus topic subscription azr-fleet-platform-events/automation/catchall correlated visible topic messages to governance callback 404s in azr-compliance-v3. Catchall active backlog had 3697 messages and 1223 dead-letter messages; dead-letter items were only TTLExpiredException from 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-05, unrelated to the incident. Incident-hour correlation: 2026-03-17T02:00Z had 4 catchall messages and 4 callback 404s; all 4 were policy-label AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_CREATE_REQUESTED with username FAzureProdEngAutomation. 2026-03-17T15:00Z had 14 catchall messages, of which 10 were AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_DELETE_REQUESTED with username janus.prod.2 and 4 were AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_CLOSED with null username; the 10 delete requests aligned with the 10 callback 404s in that hour. 2026-03-19T09:00Z had 437 catchall messages, all under username FAzureProdEngAutomation, composed of 432 AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_ENABLE_REQUESTED and 5 AZURE_GOVERNANCE_RULE_DELETE_REQUESTED; this matched 437 callback 404s in the same hour. Representative message bodies included data.username and data.jobId, showing real automation work items rather than random replay noise. Conclusion: the callback 404s correlate directly to automation topic messages, specifically governance rule create/enable/delete operations from the Fleet platform automation path, and not to unrelated WAF/APIM issues.

--- Episode 4 [codex 2026-03-20] (similarity: 0.5363) ---
Webhook incident on March 20, 2026: prod webhook ingress was healthy and Service Bus auth worked, but main function apps in dev/prod were missing the new resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger live even though code/app settings had been updated. Root cause was trigger metadata not synced for the main app after adding a new Service Bus trigger; manual POST to Microsoft.Web/sites/<app>/syncfunctiontriggers immediately surfaced resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger in both dev and prod. After sync, resource-change-ingest messages drained but failed with ValueError 'Invalid resource-change ingest message: no valid events in batch' when every event in a raw-ingest batch was unsupported for business reasons (for example unknown subscription/token bundle). This caused 10 retries and DLQ. Local code fix: in _start_resource_change_ingest_orchestration, keep raising for malformed message/schema errors, but when invalid_events > 0 and run_ids is empty, log and return '' instead of raising, so unsupported batches are acknowledged instead of DLQ-retried.

--- Episode 5 [codex 2026-03-20] (similarity: 0.5343) ---
On 2026-03-20 during prod health validation of fleet-platform-app after PR #59 deployment, azr-fleet-platform-prod main app was healthy but azr-fleet-webhook-prod was in an active outage. Public and direct webhook health endpoints returned 500, while direct main /api/health returned 200. App Insights over the prior 30 minutes showed about 71,770 webhook requests with about 70,524 failures. Dominant exceptions were System.InvalidOperationException 'Did not find any initialized language workers' and FunctionTimeoutException at 00:00:30 for Functions.eventgrid_resource_change_webhook. 5-minute bins showed webhook p95 around 32s from 19:10-19:35 UTC. Service Bus namespace azr-fleet-platform-events showed no ServerErrors/UserErrors/ThrottledRequests, and ServerSendLatency averaged roughly 307-763 ms, so namespace-side failure was not the primary cause. Root-cause inference: current webhook code opens and closes a Service Bus sender per request and only wraps sender.send_messages() in wait_for; sender acquisition/teardown is outside the timeout guard, allowing requests to sit until the Functions host kills workers. A local hotfix was prepared to use a reusable sender pool per worker process and bound the entire acquire-plus-send operation.

--- Episode 6 [codex 2026-04-01] (similarity: 0.5329) ---
April 1, 2026 prod App Insights triage for fleet platform: main app azr-fleet-platform-prod showed no active exceptions or failed requests in the queried 24h window for resource_change_ingest_sb_trigger after prior fixes. Remaining active failures were in azr-fleet-webhook-prod: 4 recent WebhookEnqueueFailure 503s caused by transient Microsoft.ServiceBus send timeouts during sender.send_messages. Patched functions-webhook/function_app.py to retry once on transient send timeout using a fresh retired transport/client while keeping the same overall operation timeout budget, and added regression tests in tests/test_webhook_token_validation.py. Also added the missing _SERVICEBUS_CLIENT_LOCK global declaration.

--- Episode 7 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.5317) ---
For the 2026-03-19 azr-compliance-v3 prod callback 404 investigation, the python-httpx client was traced to the external Fleet Platform prod function app, not the compliance repo backend. Evidence: the shared ASE outbound IP 172.206.172.169 is used by multiple apps, but azr-fleet-platform-prod and azr-fleet-webhook-prod are the only prod Fleet Python function apps. The automation Service Bus topic in namespace azr-fleet-platform-events has semantic subscriptions policy/alerts/ama/tags; the policy subscription is filtered on label=policy, matching the compliance app's documented bulk automation publish path. azr-fleet-platform-prod exposes serviceBusTrigger functions automation_policies_topic_trigger and an activity function send_bulk_operation_callback. AppTraces for AppRoleName=azr-fleet-platform-prod show repeated 'Executing Functions.send_bulk_operation_callback' and 'HTTP Request: POST https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback "HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found"' messages during the incident window. azr-fleet-webhook-prod showed no matching dependency/callback telemetry and only hosts webhook ingress functions.

--- Episode 8 [codex 2026-04-03] (similarity: 0.5314) ---
On 2026-04-03 in fleet-platform-app prod telemetry, investigated two incidents during a 60-minute deployment watch. Main app incident: between 17:33:55Z and 17:34:20Z, send_bulk_operation_callback failed with RetryableHTTPError HTTP 500 after 4 attempts from BULK_AUTOMATION_CALLBACK_URL=https://governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback. Multiple distinct returned correlationIds were observed (including 9e3e8b0f-43ce-4c05-8885-1c8b48dc88be and 7c189fbd-4a41-45b1-af1e-90835f0874c2). This maps to policy_rule_events.py callback send path and bulk_callback_client/http_client retry behavior, but prod-azr-compliance-v3 App Insights showed 164 BulkOperationCallback requests in the same 17:33:30Z-17:35:30Z window and all were HTTP 200, with no visible backend 500s. Conclusion: likely external governance/policy-engine/APIM layer issue in front of the callback backend, not a fleet-platform internal fault after the DB migration fix. Webhook incident: at 18:19:25Z and 18:20:16Z, POST /v1/events/resource-change calls failed; APIM client-side requests cut off at ~20s and backend app requests ended as 500 after ~44s with FunctionTimeoutAbortException. Request-scoped webhook traces showed ServiceBusAuthenticationError / 'CBS Authentication Expired' during the enqueue path. Current webhook code has _servicebus_operation_timeout_seconds plus sender.send_messages timeout, but _servicebus_sender_context cleanup awaits _close_sender_slot and _retire_servicebus_transport without timeout. No WebhookEnqueueFailure trace was emitted for the failed operation IDs, suggesting the request got stuck during Service Bus cleanup/cancellation before it could return the intended 503 response.

--- Episode 9 [codex 2026-04-15] (similarity: 0.5287) ---
On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected VpnOnly blocks from external/scanner or preview IPs such as 146.70.189.237 and 52.123.190.89/125. The fazure-mcp backend is App Service azure-mcp-prod in rg-azure-mcp-prod with linked App Insights azure-mcp-prod. App Service state was Running and App Gateway show-backend-health reported the backend healthy. App Service metrics showed Requests and Http2xx continuing through the window and Http5xx stayed 0 throughout, confirming the backend was not returning server 500s. App Gateway metrics showed HealthyHostCount stayed 1 and UnhealthyHostCount stayed 0 throughout the incident while FailedRequests spiked, indicating short transient backend unavailability that did not trip health-state changes. Critical config finding: the App Gateway probe for fazure-mcp-health uses host azure-mcp-prod.prodengineeringase.p.azurewebsites.net path /health interval 300 seconds timeout 30 unhealthyThreshold 3, while the other fazure probes use 30-second intervals. The backend http setting fazure-mcp-backend uses HTTPS/443 requestTimeout 20. Azure activity log for azure-mcp-prod showed manual Restart Web App operations initiated by daniel.temalatzi@rackspace.com from 72.32.180.178 at 2026-04-15 14:47:18Z and again at 15:14:16Z. The large 14:38-14:51 502 burst overlapped the 14:47 restart. Web app config also showed numberOfWorkers=1 and no App Service healthCheckPath configured. Conclusion: the dominant user-facing errors were 502s caused by backend restarts/recycles on a single-worker App Service, with App Gateway keeping the backend marked healthy because the custom probe interval is 300s. Recommended fixes: reduce fazure-mcp App Gateway probe interval from 300s to 30s like the other services, avoid manual restarts during business traffic, and add backend redundancy or deployment strategy (additional worker/instance or slot/blue-green) because any single-worker restart causes downtime. A separate activity-log event at 2026-04-15 13:58:05Z showed App Service managed certificate renewal failure for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because DNS points to the App Gateway IP 20.118.70.227 instead of the App Service IP; this is operationally notable but not the cause of the observed 502/403 issue.

--- Episode 10 [codex 2026-03-19] (similarity: 0.5193) ---
In azr-compliance-v3 prod callback 404 investigation on 2026-03-19, APIM and App Insights confirmed the 451 bulk callback 404s were not gateway misses or WAF blocks. AGWAccessLogs showed one client IP 172.206.172.169 with user agent python-httpx/0.28.1 hitting governance.mscloud.rackspace.com/policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback and receiving 404. AGWFirewallLogs showed Action=Allowed with custom rule AllowHeaders matching the Host header, not a block. ApiManagementGatewayLogs showed APIM forwarded POST /policy-engine/v2/bulk-operations/callback to backend URL https://prod-azr-compliance-v3.scus-prd-ase-janus-azure.p.azurewebsites.net/api/bulk-operations/callback and received backend 404 for all 451 requests, with APIM CallerIpAddress values 192.168.1.68 (390) and 192.168.1.69 (61). AppRequests/AppTraces confirmed BulkOperationCallback executed and returned 404, but only logged 'Processing callback'; no matching-item-not-found detail is logged. App Insights marks these 404 request rows as Success=True, so use ResultCode instead of Success for this path.
